| summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by IncidentNumber
| where Status == "Closed" and isnotempty(ClosedTime)
| where ClosedTime >= start and ClosedTime < end
| extend Month = format_datetime(ClosedTime, "yyyy-MM")
| extend ResponderRaw = coalesce(tostring(Owner.assignedTo), tostring(Owner.userPrincipalName))
| where isnotempty(ResponderRaw) and ResponderRaw !in~ ("Unassigned")
| extend Responder = iff(ResponderRaw contains "@", tostring(split(ResponderRaw, "@")[0]), ResponderRaw)
| summarize ClosedIncidents = dcount(IncidentNumber) by Month, Responder, Title
| order by Month asc, Responder asc, ClosedIncidents desc

name: Single Responder Monthly Trend
desc: Tracks a specific responder's monthly closed incident count over a year. Useful for individual performance tracking and 1:1 review preparation.
kql:
// NOTE: Replace accountid with the responder's account name and adjust dates
let start = datetime(2025-01-01);
let end   = datetime(2026-01-01);
let responder = "accountid";
SecurityIncident
| where Status == "Closed"
| where isnotempty(CreatedTime) and isnotempty(ClosedTime)
| where ClosedTime >= start and ClosedTime < end
| extend Month = format_datetime(ClosedTime, "yyyy-MM")
| extend ResponderRaw = coalesce(tostring(Owner.assignedTo), tostring(Owner.userPrincipalName))
| where isnotempty(ResponderRaw) and ResponderRaw !in~ ("Unassigned")
| extend Responder = iff(ResponderRaw contains "@", tostring(split(ResponderRaw, "@")[0]), ResponderRaw)
| where Responder =~ responder
| summarize ClosedCount = count() by Month
| order by Month asc

name: Top 10 Closed Incident Types
desc: Shows the top 10 most frequently closed incident types (by title/detection rule name). Helps identify which detections generate the most work and may be candidates for automation or tuning.

name: Yearly Responder MTTR Performance
desc: Annual MTTR performance per responder with closed count, average/P50/P90 resolution times. Designed for year-end SOC performance reviews and identifying training needs.
kql:
// NOTE: Adjust start and end dates to your reporting period
let monthStart = datetime(2025-01-01);
let monthEnd   = datetime(2026-01-01);
SecurityIncident
| where Status == "Closed"
| where isnotempty(CreatedTime) and isnotempty(ClosedTime)
| where ClosedTime >= monthStart and ClosedTime < monthEnd
| extend ResponderRaw = coalesce(tostring(Owner.assignedTo), tostring(Owner.userPrincipalName))
| where isnotempty(ResponderRaw) and ResponderRaw !in~ ("Unassigned")
| extend Responder = iff(ResponderRaw contains "@", tostring(split(ResponderRaw, "@")[0]), ResponderRaw)
| extend ResolveMinutes = (ClosedTime - CreatedTime) / 1m
| summarize
    ClosedCount = count(),
    MTTR_Min = avg(ResolveMinutes),
    P50_Min  = percentile(ResolveMinutes, 50),
    P90_Min  = percentile(ResolveMinutes, 90)
  by Responder
| order by ClosedCount desc

name: OAuth Consent and App Registration Summary
desc: Summarizes all OAuth consent grants, app role assignments, permission grants, new app registrations and service principal additions over 7 days. Shows which apps received consent, how many times and by whom. Broader than illicit consent detection. Covers all consent activity for governance review.
kql:
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where OperationName has_any ("Consent to application", "Add app role assignment", "Add OAuth2PermissionGrant", "Add application", "Add service principal")
| extend InitiatedBy = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName)
| extend TargetApp = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| extend TargetAppId = tostring(TargetResources[0].id)
| summarize 
    ConsentCount = count(),
    LastConsent = max(TimeGenerated),
    Users = make_set(InitiatedBy, 10)
    by OperationName, TargetApp, TargetAppId
| order by ConsentCount desc

name: Active Account Password Age Report
desc: Comprehensive password age report for all active (non-disabled) accounts combining MDI IdentityAccountInfo and IdentityInfo tables. Shows days/years since last password change, PasswordNeverExpires and PasswordNotRequired flags, sensitive account tagging and OU path. Requires MDI.
kql:
let accountinfo = IdentityAccountInfo
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated,*) by IdentityId
| extend DaysSinceLastPasswordChange =
    iff(isnull(LastPasswordChangeTime), int(null),
        datetime_diff('day', now(), LastPasswordChangeTime))
| extend YearsSinceLastPasswordChange =
    iff(isnull(LastPasswordChangeTime), int(null),
        datetime_diff('year', now(), LastPasswordChangeTime))   
| extend Sensitive = array_index_of(Tags, "Sensitive") != -1
| extend SensitiveLabel = iff(Sensitive == 1, "Sensitive", "Not Sensitive")
| project IdentityId, AccountUpn, AccountStatus, LastPasswordChangeTime, DaysSinceLastPasswordChange, YearsSinceLastPasswordChange, Sid, Sensitive, SensitiveLabel;
let IdInfo = IdentityInfo
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated,*) by IdentityId
| extend PasswordNeverExpires = array_index_of(UserAccountControl, "PasswordNeverExpires") != -1,
         PasswordNotRequired = array_index_of(UserAccountControl, "PasswordNotRequired") != -1
| extend OUPath = extract(@"CN=[^,]+,(.*)", 1, DistinguishedName)
| project IdentityId, AccountName, AccountDomain, AccountDisplayName, OnPremSid, OnPremObjectId, AccountUpn, PasswordNeverExpires, PasswordNotRequired, Type, OUPath;
IdInfo
| join kind=leftouter (accountinfo) on .IdentityId == .IdentityId
| project IdentityId, AccountName, AccountStatus, AccountDomain, AccountDisplayName, AccountUpn, Sensitive, SensitiveLabel, LastPasswordChangeTime, DaysSinceLastPasswordChange, YearsSinceLastPasswordChange, PasswordNeverExpires, PasswordNotRequired, Type, OUPath
| sort by DaysSinceLastPasswordChange desc 
| where AccountStatus != "Disabled"

name: Password Age Distribution by Bucket
desc: Groups active accounts into password age buckets (0-1 years, 1-2 years, 2-5 years, 5-10 years, 10+ years). Provides a clear view of password hygiene across the organization. Essential for compliance reporting and identifying accounts with dangerously old passwords. Requires MDI.
kql:
let accountinfo = IdentityAccountInfo
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated,*) by IdentityId
| extend DaysSinceLastPasswordChange =
    iff(isnull(LastPasswordChangeTime), int(null),
        datetime_diff('day', now(), LastPasswordChangeTime))
| project IdentityId, AccountUpn, AccountStatus, LastPasswordChangeTime, DaysSinceLastPasswordChange;
let IdInfo = IdentityInfo
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated,*) by IdentityId
| project IdentityId, AccountName;
IdInfo
| join kind=leftouter (accountinfo) on .IdentityId == .IdentityId
| where AccountStatus != "Disabled"
| where isnotnull(LastPasswordChangeTime)
| extend PasswordAgeBucket =
    case(
        DaysSinceLastPasswordChange <= 365, "0-1 years",
        DaysSinceLastPasswordChange <= 2*365, "1-2 years",
        DaysSinceLastPasswordChange <= 5*365, "2-5 years",
        DaysSinceLastPasswordChange <= 10*365, "5-10 years",
        "10+ years")
| summarize Accounts = dcount(IdentityId) by PasswordAgeBucket
| sort by Accounts desc

name: ChatGPT Enterprise Sign-In Monitoring
desc: Monitors all sign-in activity to the ChatGPT Enterprise application (AppID: e0476654-c1d5-430b-ab80-70cbd947616a) over 180 days. Shows which users accessed ChatGPT, from where, how Conditional Access evaluated the attempt and whether MFA was required. Essential for AI governance.
kql:
let ChatGPTAppId = "e0476654-c1d5-430b-ab80-70cbd947616a";
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(180d)
| where AppId == ChatGPTAppId
| extend
    Country = tostring(LocationDetails.countryOrRegion),
    City    = tostring(LocationDetails.city)
| project
    TimeGenerated,
    UserDisplayName,
    UserPrincipalName,
    IPAddress,
    Country,
    City,
    ConditionalAccessStatus,
    ClientAppUsed,
    AuthenticationRequirement,
    ResultType,
    ResultDescription
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: Entra ID Admin Role Assignments
desc: Lists all users with Entra ID admin role assignments from the last 30 days. Shows which accounts hold which administrative roles. Essential for privileged access reviews, least privilege enforcement and identifying over-privileged accounts.
kql:
IdentityInfo
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where AssignedRoles != ""
| mv-expand AssignedRoles
| extend EntraAdminRole = tostring(AssignedRoles)
| project Timestamp, EntraAdminRole, AccountDisplayName
| where AccountDisplayName != ""
| sort by EntraAdminRole asc, Timestamp desc

name: Weak MFA Method Registration Detection
desc: Detects when users register or are configured with weak MFA methods: voice call (mobile/office/other), SMS (two-way/one-way). These methods are vulnerable to SIM swapping, SS7 attacks and social engineering. Any registration of weak methods should be investigated. Could indicate an attacker downgrading MFA to bypass stronger methods.
kql:
let WeakMethods = dynamic([0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5]);
let WeakMethodNames = dynamic(["TwoWayVoiceMobile","TwoWaySms","TwoWayVoiceOffice","TwoWayVoiceOtherMobile","TwoWaySmsOtherMobile","OneWaySms"]);
CloudAppEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType == "Update user."
| where RawEventData contains "StrongAuthenticationMethod"
| extend Target = tostring(RawEventData.ObjectId)
| extend Actor = tostring(RawEventData.UserId)
| mv-expand ModifiedProperties = parse_json(RawEventData.ModifiedProperties)
| where ModifiedProperties.Name == "StrongAuthenticationMethod"
| extend NewValue = parse_json(tostring(ModifiedProperties.NewValue))
| mv-apply NewValue on (
    extend MethodType = toint(NewValue.MethodType)
    | where MethodType in (WeakMethods)
    | summarize AddedWeakMethods = make_set(strcat(MethodType, ": ", iff(MethodType < array_length(WeakMethodNames), tostring(WeakMethodNames[MethodType]), "Unknown")))
)
| where array_length(AddedWeakMethods) > 0
| extend AddedWeakMethodsStr = tostring(AddedWeakMethods)
| project Timestamp, Actor, Target, AddedWeakMethodsStr, ReportId
| distinct *
| order by Timestamp desc

name: Sensitive Accounts List (Exposure Graph)
desc: Lists all user accounts tagged as Sensitive in Microsoft Security Exposure Management, along with their nested AD group memberships. Sensitive accounts are high-value targets. This query helps verify the sensitive account list is accurate and complete.
kql:
ExposureGraphNodes
| where NodeLabel == 'user'
| extend Tags = parse_json(tostring(parse_json(tostring(NodeProperties.rawData)).tags.[0]))
| extend NestedADGroups = parse_json(tostring(parse_json(tostring(NodeProperties.rawData)).nestedAdGroupNames))
| mv-expand NestedADGroups
| where Tags == 'Sensitive'

name: Temporary Access Pass (TAP) Usage
desc: Tracks all Temporary Access Pass creation and usage events. TAPs are time-limited credentials used for onboarding or recovery. Unauthorized TAP creation could indicate an attacker bypassing standard authentication to gain access to an account.
kql:
AuditLogs
| where Result == "success"
| where ResultDescription has "temporary access pass"
| project TimeGenerated, ResultDescription, OperationName, Identity, InitiatedBy, TargetResources
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: User URL Click Investigation
desc: Investigates all URLs clicked by specific target users that were allowed through Safe Links. Shows URL domains, click counts and timestamps. Useful for post-incident investigation when you need to know exactly what a compromised user clicked on.
kql:
// NOTE: Replace account@domain.com with the user(s) you want to investigate
let TargetUsers = dynamic(["account@domain.com"]);
UrlClickEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountUpn in~ (TargetUsers)
| where ActionType == "ClickAllowed"
| extend UrlDomain = extract("https?://([^/]+)", 1, Url)
| summarize 
    ClickedBy = make_set(AccountUpn),
    ClickedByCount = dcount(AccountUpn),
    ClickTimes = make_set(Timestamp),
    TotalClicks = count()
    by Url, UrlDomain, NetworkMessageId
| order by ClickedByCount desc, TotalClicks desc

name: Azure Activity - Resource Group Deletion
desc: Detects deletion of Azure resource groups which could indicate destructive activity or unauthorized cleanup.
kql:
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where OperationNameValue == "MICROSOFT.RESOURCES/SUBSCRIPTIONS/RESOURCEGROUPS/DELETE"
| where ActivityStatusValue == "Success"
| project TimeGenerated, Caller, CallerIpAddress, ResourceGroup, SubscriptionId
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: Azure Activity - Role Assignment Changes
desc: Tracks Azure RBAC role assignment creations and deletions to detect privilege escalation or unauthorized access grants.
kql:
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where OperationNameValue has_any ("MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/ROLEASSIGNMENTS/WRITE", "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/ROLEASSIGNMENTS/DELETE")
| where ActivityStatusValue == "Success"
| project TimeGenerated, Caller, CallerIpAddress, OperationNameValue, ResourceGroup, SubscriptionId
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: Azure Activity - Policy Assignment Changes
desc: Monitors Azure Policy assignment changes which could weaken security posture if compliance policies are removed.
kql:
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where OperationNameValue has_any ("MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/POLICYASSIGNMENTS/WRITE", "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/POLICYASSIGNMENTS/DELETE")
| where ActivityStatusValue == "Success"
| project TimeGenerated, Caller, CallerIpAddress, OperationNameValue, ResourceGroup, SubscriptionId
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: Azure Activity - Subscription Operations by External Caller
desc: Identifies subscription-level write or delete operations initiated by callers outside the primary tenant domain.
kql:
// NOTE: Replace domain.com with your organization's primary domain
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where CategoryValue == "Administrative"
| where ActivityStatusValue == "Success"
| where Caller !has "domain.com"
| where isnotempty(Caller)
| where OperationNameValue has_any ("WRITE", "DELETE")
| summarize OperationCount = count(), Operations = make_set(OperationNameValue) by Caller, CallerIpAddress
| order by OperationCount desc

name: Azure Activity - NSG Rule Modifications
desc: Detects changes to Network Security Group rules which could expose resources to unauthorized network access.
kql:
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where OperationNameValue has "MICROSOFT.NETWORK/NETWORKSECURITYGROUPS"
| where OperationNameValue has_any ("WRITE", "DELETE")
| where ActivityStatusValue == "Success"
| project TimeGenerated, Caller, CallerIpAddress, OperationNameValue, ResourceGroup, Resource
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: Azure Activity - Diagnostic Setting Removal
desc: Detects removal of diagnostic settings from Azure resources. Attackers disable logging to cover their tracks and avoid detection.
kql:
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where OperationNameValue has "MICROSOFT.INSIGHTS/DIAGNOSTICSETTINGS/DELETE"
| where ActivityStatusValue == "Success"
| project TimeGenerated, Caller, CallerIpAddress, Resource, ResourceGroup
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: Azure Activity - Virtual Machine Deletion
desc: Monitors Azure VM deletion events for detecting destructive activities or unauthorized resource removal.
kql:
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where OperationNameValue == "MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINES/DELETE"
| where ActivityStatusValue == "Success"
| project TimeGenerated, Caller, CallerIpAddress, Resource, ResourceGroup, SubscriptionId
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: Azure Key Vault - Access Policy Changes
desc: Monitors changes to Key Vault access policies which could grant unauthorized access to secrets, keys and certificates.
kql:
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResourceProviderValue == "MICROSOFT.KEYVAULT"
| where OperationNameValue has_any ("VAULTS/WRITE", "VAULTS/DELETE", "VAULTS/ACCESSPOLICIES/WRITE")
| where ActivityStatusValue == "Success"
| project TimeGenerated, Caller, CallerIpAddress, OperationNameValue, Resource, ResourceGroup
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: Azure Key Vault - Secret Access Anomalies
desc: Detects unusual volume of secret retrieval operations from Azure Key Vault which may indicate credential harvesting.
kql:
AzureDiagnostics
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResourceProvider == "MICROSOFT.KEYVAULT"
| where OperationName == "SecretGet"
| summarize AccessCount = count() by CallerIPAddress, identity_claim_upn_s, Resource
| where AccessCount > 20
| order by AccessCount desc

name: Azure Key Vault - Key and Secret Deletion
desc: Detects deletion of keys or secrets from Azure Key Vault which could be part of a destructive attack or evidence tampering.
kql:
AzureDiagnostics
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResourceProvider == "MICROSOFT.KEYVAULT"
| where OperationName has_any ("SecretDelete", "KeyDelete", "CertificateDelete")
| project TimeGenerated, CallerIPAddress, identity_claim_upn_s, OperationName, Resource, ResultType
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: Azure Key Vault - Access from New IP Address
desc: Identifies Key Vault access from IP addresses not seen in the previous 14 days. Useful for detecting compromised credentials accessing secrets.
kql:
let KnownIPs =
    AzureDiagnostics
    | where TimeGenerated between (ago(14d) .. ago(1d))
    | where ResourceProvider == "MICROSOFT.KEYVAULT"
    | where isnotempty(CallerIPAddress)
    | distinct CallerIPAddress;
AzureDiagnostics
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1d)
| where ResourceProvider == "MICROSOFT.KEYVAULT"
| where CallerIPAddress !in (KnownIPs)
| where isnotempty(CallerIPAddress)
| summarize Operations = make_set(OperationName), Count = count() by CallerIPAddress, identity_claim_upn_s, Resource
| order by Count desc

name: DNS - High Volume NXDOMAIN Responses
desc: Detects endpoints generating a high volume of NXDOMAIN responses which may indicate DGA-based malware or DNS tunneling reconnaissance.
kql:
DnsEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1d)
| where ResultCode == 3
| summarize NXDOMAINCount = count(), UniqueDomains = dcount(Name) by ClientIP
| where NXDOMAINCount > 500
| order by NXDOMAINCount desc

name: DNS - Queries to Suspicious Top Level Domains
desc: Identifies DNS queries to uncommon TLDs often abused by threat actors for phishing and C2 infrastructure.
kql:
let SuspiciousTLDs = dynamic([".xyz", ".top", ".buzz", ".club", ".icu", ".cam", ".surf", ".rest", ".monster"]);
DnsEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1d)
| where Name has_any (SuspiciousTLDs)
| summarize QueryCount = count(), UniqueClients = dcount(ClientIP) by Name
| order by QueryCount desc

name: DNS - Long Subdomain Queries (Potential Tunneling)
desc: Detects DNS queries with unusually long subdomain labels which is a common indicator of DNS tunneling for data exfiltration.
kql:
DnsEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1d)
| extend SubdomainLength = strlen(tostring(split(Name, ".")[0]))
| where SubdomainLength > 40
| summarize QueryCount = count() by ClientIP, Name
| order by QueryCount desc

name: DNS - TXT Record Query Anomalies
desc: Monitors for unusual volumes of DNS TXT record queries which are commonly used in DNS tunneling and C2 communications.
kql:
DnsEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1d)
| where QueryType == 16
| summarize TXTCount = count(), UniqueDomains = dcount(Name) by ClientIP
| where TXTCount > 50
| order by TXTCount desc

name: UEBA - Users with Multiple Risk Indicators
desc: Identifies users flagged by UEBA with multiple concurrent risk indicators such as anomalous activity patterns and risk scores.
kql:
BehaviorAnalytics
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where isnotempty(UserPrincipalName)
| summarize
    RiskCount = count(),
    Activities = make_set(ActivityType),
    MaxScore = max(InvestigationPriority)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where RiskCount > 3
| order by MaxScore desc

name: UEBA - First Time User Accessed Resource
desc: Detects users accessing resources they have never accessed before based on UEBA behavioral baselines.
kql:
BehaviorAnalytics
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ActivityInsights has "FirstTimeUserAccessedResource"
| project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, ActivityType, SourceIPAddress, ActivityInsights, InvestigationPriority
| order by InvestigationPriority desc

name: UEBA - Anomalous Login Then Privileged Operation
desc: Correlates UEBA anomalous sign-in events with subsequent privileged operations to identify potential compromised accounts performing lateral movement.
kql:
let AnomalousUsers =
    BehaviorAnalytics
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(1d)
    | where ActivityType == "LogOn"
    | where InvestigationPriority > 5
    | distinct UserPrincipalName;
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1d)
| extend UPN = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy.user)).userPrincipalName)
| where UPN in (AnomalousUsers)
| where OperationName has_any ("Add member to role", "Add owner", "Add app role assignment")
| project TimeGenerated, UPN, OperationName, TargetResources
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: UEBA - Unusual Country Sign-In Activity
desc: Identifies sign-in activity from countries not previously seen in UEBA behavioral baselines for the user.
kql:
BehaviorAnalytics
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ActivityInsights has "FirstTimeUserConnectedFromCountry"
| project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, SourceIPAddress, ActivityInsights, InvestigationPriority
| order by InvestigationPriority desc

name: Defender for Cloud Apps - Mass File Download
desc: Detects users downloading an unusually high number of files from cloud applications which may indicate data exfiltration or insider threat activity.
kql:
CloudAppEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(1d)
| where ActionType == "FileDownloaded"
| summarize DownloadCount = count(), UniqueFiles = dcount(ObjectName) by AccountDisplayName, AccountObjectId
| where DownloadCount > 50
| order by DownloadCount desc

name: Defender for Cloud Apps - OAuth App Consent via CloudAppEvents
desc: Identifies OAuth application consent grants via CloudAppEvents table. Complements the AuditLogs-based illicit consent detection with XDR-native visibility.
kql:
CloudAppEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType == "Consent to application."
| extend AppName = tostring(RawEventData.Target[3].ID)
| project Timestamp, AccountDisplayName, AppName, IPAddress, ActionType
| order by Timestamp desc

name: Defender for Cloud Apps - Sharing to External Domains
desc: Monitors file sharing events to external email domains in cloud applications to detect potential data leakage or unauthorized sharing.
kql:
// NOTE: Replace domain.com with your organization's primary domain
CloudAppEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType has_any ("SharingSet", "AddedToSharingLink", "FileShared")
| extend SharedWith = tostring(RawEventData)
| where SharedWith !has "domain.com"
| project Timestamp, AccountDisplayName, ActionType, ObjectName, Application
| order by Timestamp desc

name: Defender for Cloud Apps - Login from Anonymous Proxy
desc: Detects cloud application sign-ins originating from anonymous proxies or Tor exit nodes indicating potential evasion techniques.
kql:
CloudAppEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType == "UserLoggedIn"
| where RawEventData has_any ("anonymous", "anonymousProxy")
| project Timestamp, AccountDisplayName, IPAddress, Application, ActionType
| order by Timestamp desc

name: Conditional Access - Policy Disabled or Deleted
desc: Detects when a Conditional Access policy is disabled or deleted which could weaken the organization's access control posture.
kql:
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where OperationName has_any ("Update conditional access policy", "Delete conditional access policy")
| extend Actor = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy.user)).userPrincipalName)
| extend PolicyName = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| extend ModifiedProperties = TargetResources[0].modifiedProperties
| project TimeGenerated, Actor, OperationName, PolicyName, ModifiedProperties
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: Conditional Access - Policy Changed to Report-Only
desc: Identifies Conditional Access policies switched from enforced to report-only mode which effectively disables enforcement while appearing active.
kql:
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where OperationName == "Update conditional access policy"
| extend Actor = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy.user)).userPrincipalName)
| extend PolicyName = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| mv-expand ModifiedProperty = TargetResources[0].modifiedProperties
| where tostring(ModifiedProperty.displayName) == "State"
| where tostring(ModifiedProperty.newValue) has "enabledForReportingButNotEnforced"
| project TimeGenerated, Actor, PolicyName, OldValue = tostring(ModifiedProperty.oldValue), NewValue = tostring(ModifiedProperty.newValue)
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: Conditional Access - Exclusion Modifications
desc: Detects modifications to Conditional Access policy exclusions. Attackers may modify exclusions to bypass MFA requirements.
kql:
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where OperationName has_any ("Add conditional access policy", "Update conditional access policy")
| extend Actor = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy.user)).userPrincipalName)
| extend PolicyName = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| mv-expand ModifiedProperty = TargetResources[0].modifiedProperties
| where tostring(ModifiedProperty.displayName) has_any ("ExcludeUsers", "ExcludeGroups")
| project TimeGenerated, Actor, OperationName, PolicyName, ModifiedProperty
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: Conditional Access - Named Location Changes
desc: Monitors changes to Named Locations in Conditional Access. Modifying trusted locations could allow attackers to bypass location-based access restrictions.
kql:
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where OperationName has_any ("Add named location", "Update named location", "Delete named location")
| extend Actor = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy.user)).userPrincipalName)
| extend LocationName = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| project TimeGenerated, Actor, OperationName, LocationName, TargetResources
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: Conditional Access - Sign-In Failures Due to Policy Block
desc: Summarizes sign-in failures caused by Conditional Access policy blocks (ResultType 53003). Helps identify misconfigured policies or persistent attack attempts being stopped.
kql:
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResultType == 53003
| summarize BlockCount = count() by UserPrincipalName, AppDisplayName, IPAddress
| order by BlockCount desc

name: PIM - Role Activation Outside Business Hours
desc: Detects PIM role activations occurring outside standard business hours which may indicate compromised accounts or unauthorized privilege escalation.
kql:
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where OperationName == "Add member to role completed (PIM activation)"
| extend HourOfDay = datetime_part("hour", TimeGenerated)
| where HourOfDay < 7 or HourOfDay > 20
| extend Actor = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy.user)).userPrincipalName)
| extend RoleName = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| project TimeGenerated, Actor, RoleName, HourOfDay
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: PIM - Frequent Role Activations by Single User
desc: Identifies users activating PIM roles at an unusually high frequency which could indicate abuse of just-in-time access or automation of privilege escalation.
kql:
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where OperationName == "Add member to role completed (PIM activation)"
| extend Actor = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy.user)).userPrincipalName)
| extend RoleName = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| summarize ActivationCount = count(), Roles = make_set(RoleName) by Actor
| where ActivationCount > 10
| order by ActivationCount desc

name: PIM - Global Administrator Activation Tracker
desc: Tracks all Global Administrator PIM activations. This is the highest privilege role in Entra ID and every activation should be verified.
kql:
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where OperationName == "Add member to role completed (PIM activation)"
| extend Actor = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy.user)).userPrincipalName)
| extend RoleName = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| where RoleName == "Global Administrator"
| project TimeGenerated, Actor, RoleName, CorrelationId
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: PIM - Permanent Role Assignment (Bypassing PIM)
desc: Detects permanent (active) role assignments bypassing PIM eligibility requirements. Permanent assignments undermine just-in-time access controls.
kql:
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where OperationName has "Add member to role"
| where OperationName !has "PIM"
| extend Actor = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy.user)).userPrincipalName)
| extend RoleName = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| extend Target = tostring(TargetResources[2].userPrincipalName)
| project TimeGenerated, Actor, Target, RoleName, OperationName
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: PIM - Activation with Suspicious Justification
desc: Reviews PIM activation justification text to detect generic or suspicious reasons. Short or repetitive justifications may indicate unauthorized use.
kql:
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where OperationName == "Add member to role completed (PIM activation)"
| extend Actor = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy.user)).userPrincipalName)
| extend RoleName = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| mv-expand AdditionalDetail = AdditionalDetails
| where tostring(AdditionalDetail.key) == "Justification"
| extend Justification = tostring(AdditionalDetail.value)
| where strlen(Justification) < 5 or Justification has_any ("test", "asdf", "123")
| project TimeGenerated, Actor, RoleName, Justification
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: PIM - Guest Account Added to PIM Role
desc: Detects when a B2B guest account is assigned to a PIM-eligible or PIM-active role. Guest accounts with privileged roles represent a significant security risk.
kql:
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where OperationName has_any ("Add eligible member to role in PIM", "Add member to role in PIM")
| extend Actor = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy.user)).userPrincipalName)
| extend Target = tostring(TargetResources[2].userPrincipalName)
| extend RoleName = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| where Target has "#EXT#"
| project TimeGenerated, Actor, Target, RoleName
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: B2B Guest - Invitation Spike Detection
desc: Detects a spike in B2B guest user invitations which may indicate a compromised account mass-inviting external collaborators.
kql:
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1d)
| where OperationName == "Invite external user"
| extend Actor = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy.user)).userPrincipalName)
| summarize InviteCount = count() by Actor
| where InviteCount > 10
| order by InviteCount desc

name: B2B Guest - Sign-Ins to Sensitive Applications
desc: Monitors guest user sign-ins to sensitive applications such as Azure Portal, Exchange Online Admin and Graph Explorer.
kql:
let SensitiveApps = dynamic(["Azure Portal", "Microsoft Azure PowerShell", "Graph Explorer", "Exchange Online PowerShell", "Azure Active Directory PowerShell"]);
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where UserType == "Guest"
| where ResultType == 0
| where AppDisplayName in (SensitiveApps)
| project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, AppDisplayName, IPAddress, Location
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: B2B Guest - Added to Privileged Group
desc: Detects B2B guest accounts being added to privileged or security-sensitive groups in Entra ID.
kql:
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where OperationName == "Add member to group"
| extend Actor = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy.user)).userPrincipalName)
| extend TargetUPN = tostring(TargetResources[0].userPrincipalName)
| extend GroupName = tostring(TargetResources[2].displayName)
| where TargetUPN has "#EXT#"
| project TimeGenerated, Actor, TargetUPN, GroupName
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: B2B Guest - Account Converted to Member
desc: Detects when a B2B guest account is converted to a member account. This privilege escalation bypasses guest access restrictions.
kql:
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where OperationName == "Update user"
| mv-expand ModifiedProperty = TargetResources[0].modifiedProperties
| where tostring(ModifiedProperty.displayName) == "UserType"
| where tostring(ModifiedProperty.oldValue) has "Guest"
| where tostring(ModifiedProperty.newValue) has "Member"
| extend Actor = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy.user)).userPrincipalName)
| extend TargetUPN = tostring(TargetResources[0].userPrincipalName)
| project TimeGenerated, Actor, TargetUPN
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: B2B Guest - Stale Guest Accounts with Recent Activity
desc: Identifies guest accounts created more than 90 days ago that have recently signed in. Long-dormant guest accounts with renewed activity warrant review.
kql:
let StaleGuests =
    IdentityInfo
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where UserType == "Guest"
    | where CreatedDateTime < ago(90d)
    | distinct AccountUPN;
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where UserPrincipalName in (StaleGuests)
| where ResultType == 0
| summarize SignInCount = count(), Apps = make_set(AppDisplayName) by UserPrincipalName, IPAddress
| order by SignInCount desc

name: Defender for Endpoint - Suspicious PowerShell Execution
desc: Detects PowerShell processes with encoded commands, download cradles or suspicious execution patterns commonly used in initial access and post-exploitation.
kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-enc", "-EncodedCommand", "IEX", "Invoke-Expression", "DownloadString", "DownloadFile", "Net.WebClient", "Start-BitsTransfer", "FromBase64String")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName
| order by Timestamp desc

name: Defender for Endpoint - LSASS Access Attempts
desc: Detects processes attempting to access LSASS memory which is a common technique for credential dumping (MITRE T1003.001).
kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName != "lsass.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("lsass", "sekurlsa", "mimikatz", "procdump")
    or (FileName in~ ("procdump.exe", "procdump64.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has "lsass")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName
| order by Timestamp desc

name: Defender for Endpoint - Suspicious Scheduled Task Creation
desc: Detects creation of scheduled tasks via schtasks.exe with suspicious patterns such as encoded commands or downloads from external URLs.
kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName =~ "schtasks.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has "/create"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("powershell", "cmd.exe", "http", "mshta", "wscript", "cscript", "certutil")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName
| order by Timestamp desc

name: Defender for Endpoint - RDP Files in Outlook Temp Folders
desc: Detects .rdp file creation in Outlook temporary folders indicating potential spear-phishing with malicious RDP attachments. Covers both Windows 10 and 11 paths.
kql:
DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName endswith ".rdp"
| where FolderPath has_any ("Content.Outlook", "Outlook\\Temp")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, FolderPath, InitiatingProcessFileName
| order by Timestamp desc

name: Defender for Endpoint - RMM Tool Execution Detection
desc: Detects execution of Remote Monitoring and Management tools commonly abused for lateral movement and C2 by threat actors.
kql:
// NOTE: Update RMMTools list based on your environment's approved software
let RMMTools = dynamic(["anydesk.exe", "teamviewer.exe", "screenconnect.exe", "connectwise.exe", "netsupportmanager.exe", "rustdesk.exe", "splashtop.exe", "atera_agent.exe", "meshagent.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName in~ (RMMTools)
| summarize ExecutionCount = count(), Devices = make_set(DeviceName) by FileName, AccountName
| order by ExecutionCount desc

name: Defender for Endpoint - LOLBin Execution Chain
desc: Detects Living-off-the-Land binary execution chains where one LOLBin spawns another. This is a common evasion technique to avoid detection.
kql:
let LOLBins = dynamic(["mshta.exe", "certutil.exe", "regsvr32.exe", "rundll32.exe", "msiexec.exe", "wmic.exe", "cscript.exe", "wscript.exe", "bitsadmin.exe", "msbuild.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName in~ (LOLBins)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (LOLBins)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine
| order by Timestamp desc

name: Defender for Endpoint - USB Storage Device Connection
desc: Detects USB removable storage device connections to endpoints. Useful for monitoring data exfiltration vectors and policy compliance.
kql:
DeviceEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType == "PnpDeviceConnected"
| extend PnPData = parse_json(AdditionalFields)
| where tostring(PnPData.ClassName) == "USB"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, PnPData
| order by Timestamp desc

name: Defender for Endpoint - Outbound Connection to Rare Port
desc: Identifies outbound network connections to uncommon ports which could indicate C2 communication or data exfiltration over non-standard channels.
kql:
let CommonPorts = dynamic([80, 443, 53, 25, 587, 993, 995, 22, 3389, 445, 8080, 8443]);
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(1d)
| where ActionType == "ConnectionSuccess"
| where RemotePort !in (CommonPorts)
| where RemoteIPType == "Public"
| summarize ConnectionCount = count(), Ports = make_set(RemotePort) by DeviceName, RemoteIP
| where ConnectionCount > 10
| order by ConnectionCount desc

name: Defender for Endpoint - ASR Rule Trigger Summary
desc: Summarizes Attack Surface Reduction rule triggers to identify endpoints and users generating the most ASR alerts for policy tuning or threat investigation.
kql:
DeviceEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType startswith "Asr"
| summarize TriggerCount = count(), Rules = make_set(ActionType) by DeviceName, AccountName
| order by TriggerCount desc

name: Defender for Endpoint - Browser Extension Side-Loading
desc: Detects browser extension installations from non-standard sources. Malicious extensions can intercept credentials and session tokens.
kql:
DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FolderPath has_any ("Extensions", "chrome-extension", "edge-extension")
| where FileName endswith ".crx" or FileName endswith ".xpi"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, FolderPath, InitiatingProcessFileName
| order by Timestamp desc

name: Defender for Endpoint - Endpoints with Multiple Device IDs
desc: Identifies endpoints associated with multiple Device IDs which may indicate improper offboarding, re-imaging issues or device duplication in the tenant.
kql:
DeviceInfo
| where Timestamp > ago(90d)
| where isnotempty(DeviceId)
| summarize DeviceCount = dcount(DeviceId) by DeviceName
| where DeviceCount > 1
| project DeviceName, DeviceCount

name: Entra ID - User Restored Then Deleted Again
desc: Detects when a deleted user is restored and then deleted again within 365 days. This pattern may indicate an attacker restoring an account for persistence and then covering tracks by re-deleting it.
kql:
// NOTE: Update whitelisted_username and whitelisted_servicePrincipalId with known automation accounts
let placeholder_defaults = dynamic({'whitelisted_username':['-'], 'whitelisted_servicePrincipalId':['-']});
let restore = AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(365d)
| where OperationName =~ "Restore user"
| extend restoreTS = TimeGenerated
| extend targetUserId = tostring(TargetResources[0].id);
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(365d)
| where OperationName =~ "Delete user"
| extend targetUserId = tostring(TargetResources[0].id)
| join kind=inner restore on $left.targetUserId == $right.targetUserId
| where restoreTS < TimeGenerated
| extend sourceUserPrincipalName = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName)
| extend sourceServicePrincipal = tostring(InitiatedBy.app.displayName)
| extend sourceServicePrincipalId = tostring(InitiatedBy.app.servicePrincipalId)
| extend sourceIpAddress = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.ipAddress)
| extend targetUserPrincipalName = tostring(TargetResources[0].userPrincipalName)
| extend whitelisted = sourceUserPrincipalName in (placeholder_defaults.whitelisted_username)
| extend whitelisted = whitelisted or sourceServicePrincipalId in (placeholder_defaults.whitelisted_servicePrincipalId)
| where not(whitelisted)
| where sourceUserPrincipalName !startswith_cs "Sync_"
| project TimeGenerated, sourceUserPrincipalName, sourceServicePrincipal, sourceIpAddress, targetUserPrincipalName, targetUserId

name: Privileged Group Membership Change (Watchlist-Based)
desc: Detects user additions to privileged Entra ID groups defined in a Sentinel Watchlist. Summarizes added users per group for rapid review of unauthorized privilege grants.

name: URL Click to Risky Sign-In Time Correlation
desc: Correlates URL click events with risky Entra ID sign-ins within a tight time window (-2min to +7min). Catches phishing clicks that lead to immediate credential compromise.
kql:
let lookbackPeriod = ago(30d);
let suspiciousUserClicks = materialize(UrlClickEvents
    | where Timestamp > lookbackPeriod
    | extend AccountUpn = tolower(AccountUpn)
    | project ClickTime = Timestamp, ActionType, UrlChain, NetworkMessageId, Url, AccountUpn);
let interestedUsersUpn = suspiciousUserClicks
    | where isnotempty(AccountUpn)
    | distinct AccountUpn;
EntraIdSignInEvents
| where Timestamp > lookbackPeriod
| where ErrorCode == 0
| where AccountUpn in~ (interestedUsersUpn)
| where RiskLevelDuringSignIn in (10, 50, 100)
| extend AccountUpn = tolower(AccountUpn)
| join kind=inner suspiciousUserClicks on AccountUpn
| where (Timestamp - ClickTime) between (-2min .. 7min)
| project Timestamp, ReportId, ClickTime, AccountUpn, RiskLevelDuringSignIn, SessionId, IPAddress, Url
